title: uraria retroflexa
intro: Uraria retroflexa, also known as the "Indian staff vine" or "Kapikachu", is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. The health benefits associated with Uraria retroflexa include the treatment of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and edema, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Uraria retroflexa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for consumption, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
